transformation_type: oxidative conversion of organosilanes to alcohols
mechanism_type: oxidation of carbon-silicon bonds through hypervalent silicon intermediates
common_conditions: activate silicon substituent or heterosilane, then oxidize with peroxide or peracid under basic or mildly acidic conditions to reveal the alcohol
description_short: Oxidative conversion of carbon-silicon bonds into alcohols, often stereospecifically and with broad functional-group tolerance in complex synthesis.

transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted acylation of aromatic or heteroaromatic substrates
common_conditions: generate the acylium electrophile with a Lewis acid, then acylate an arene or close a ring by intramolecular aromatic substitution
description_short: Classical electrophilic aromatic acylation using Lewis-acid-activated acyl donors, widely used for intermolecular substitution and intramolecular ring-closing acylation.

mechanism_type: Lewis- or Brønsted-acid-promoted carbocationic substitution on arenes
common_conditions: activate an alkyl electrophile with a Lewis or Brønsted acid and trap the resulting carbocation equivalent with an aromatic or heteroaromatic substrate
description_short: Catalytic alkylation of electron-rich arenes or heteroarenes by carbocationic electrophiles generated from halides, alkenes, or alcohol derivatives.

transformation_type: acyl migration from phenolic esters or carbamates to aromatic carbon
mechanism_type: Lewis-acid, photochemical, or anionic rearrangement to ortho/para-acylated phenols or salicylamides
common_conditions: subject phenolic esters or O-aryl carbamates to Lewis acid, light, or organolithium conditions to induce acyl transfer to the aromatic ring
description_short: Family of acyl-migration reactions that translocate acyl groups from oxygen to carbon on aromatic systems under ionic, radical, or anionic conditions.

transformation_type: conversion of alkyl electrophiles into primary amines via phthalimide masking
mechanism_type: SN2 alkylation followed by deprotection of an N-alkylphthalimide
common_conditions: alkylate phthalimide with a primary electrophile, then remove the phthalimide under hydrazine, methylamine, or hydrolytic conditions to liberate the amine
description_short: Two-step primary-amine synthesis in which phthalimide serves as a masked ammonia equivalent for SN2 alkylation and subsequent deprotection.
